element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Primary weapon throughout all stages; always available
Semi-random spawning from screen edges; spawn rate increases in western version
Eliminate to obtain secondary weapons; fewer in western version
Western version: adds 3 uses per pickup; Japanese version: adds 1 use; max 3 grenades total
Obtained from supply runners; western version adds 3 uses per pickup
Climbing mechanic; allows vertical stage traversal
More numerous in western version; fire projectiles
Descending enemies; different sprite in later stages
High threat; arcade version only (removed in NES)
Destruction reveals hidden ladders to tunnel areas (FDS only)
Special group encounters at stage conclusions

typical_relief_triggers: Completing stage and reaching boss encounter
typical_tension_triggers: Semi-random enemy spawning from screen edges (especially frequent in western version)
Increased enemy density in western version (more dangerous types)
Escalating enemy types: soldiers → specialists → jetpack/parachute units
Boss encounters at each stage end
Running out of secondary weapon ammo
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Constant low-to-moderate tension with escalating enemy pressure throughout stages; punctuated by high-tension boss encounters; westward version maintains higher baseline tension via increased spawn rates and enemy variety

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Destructible landmines reveal ladders to hidden tunnel areas (FDS version stages 2, 4, 5); tunnel sections contain standard enemies and supply runners with random exit ladder placement
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Landmine destruction in specific stages opens concealed vertical passages to underground areas

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction of basic soldiers and supply runners; knife + grenades/RPG acquisition establishes core combat loop; moderate enemy density
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Introduction of Combat Specialists, Jetpack Soldiers, and Parachute Gunners; increases aerial threats and specialized enemy types; western version maintains higher enemy spawn rates; landmine-triggered secret areas available (FDS)
difficulty_curve.late_game: Gyrocopters, Flame Throwers (arcade), and German Shepherds; stage-specific boss ambushers; final NES stages feature nuclear missile disarming objective; compressed time limits
notable_spikes: Stage 4 transition: introduction of parachute gunners and escalated enemy variety
Stage 5/Final stage: combines all previous enemy types; boss encounters; time pressure intensifies
Western version baseline spike: constant increased enemy spawn frequency and dangerous type prevalence compared to Japanese version
Boss ambusher encounters at each stage end

notable_level_patterns.pattern_name: Linear horizontal progression with vertical ladder sections
notable_level_patterns.description: Primary movement left-to-right with ladders providing vertical navigation breaks; boss ambushers at stage conclusions
Indoor environment with vertical traversal; complex enemy spacing
New stage added in NES version between Missile Base and Harbor; distinct rocket soldier enemy focus
Nuclear missile disarming objective; uses captured enemy rocket launchers; culmination stage combining all enemy types and mechanics
Accessed via landmine destruction in stages 2, 4, 5; contains standard enemies, supply runners, landmines; random exit ladder placement creates replayable variation

sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - Rush'n Attack NES/FDS)
